name: Release Management
description: Create, manage, and publish GitHub releases with auto-generated changelogs and asset attachments.

steps.title: List existing releases
steps.description: View all releases for the repository.

steps.title: Create a release with auto-generated notes
steps.description: Create a new release with automatically generated changelog from merged PRs.
steps.tip: The --generate-notes flag creates release notes from PR titles and labels since the last release.

steps.title: Create a pre-release
steps.description: Mark a release as a pre-release for beta or release candidate versions.
steps.command: gh release create v2.0.0-rc.1 --prerelease --generate-notes --title 'v2.0.0 Release Candidate 1'

steps.title: Upload assets to a release
steps.description: Attach binary files, archives, or other assets to an existing release.

steps.title: Download release assets
steps.description: Download all assets from a specific release.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Release Management
set -euo pipefail

VERSION=${1:?"Usage: $0 <version> [--prerelease]"}
PRERELEASE=${2:-""}

echo "=== Current Releases ==="
gh release list --limit 5

echo ""
echo "=== Creating Release $VERSION ==="
if [ "$PRERELEASE" = "--prerelease" ]; then
  gh release create "$VERSION" --prerelease --generate-notes --title "$VERSION"
else
  gh release create "$VERSION" --generate-notes --title "$VERSION"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Release Commands ==="
echo "View:     gh release view $VERSION"
echo "Upload:   gh release upload $VERSION ./file.tar.gz"
echo "Edit:     gh release edit $VERSION --notes 'Updated notes'"
echo "Delete:   gh release delete $VERSION"
echo "Download: gh release download $VERSION --dir ./downloads"

faq.question: How do I customize auto-generated release notes?
faq.answer: Create a .github/release.yml file to configure which labels map to which changelog categories and which contributors to exclude.

faq.question: Should I create the Git tag before or during release creation?
faq.answer: gh release create automatically creates the tag if it does not exist. If you want to sign the tag or create an annotated tag, create it manually first with git tag.
